coredata.dc:title: The sensitive “Turn-on” fluorescence platform of ascorbic acid based on conjugated polymer nanoparticles
coredata.dc:creator: Wang, Qiu-bo
Zhang, Cui-jiao
Yu, Hao
Zhang, Xian
Lu, Qian
Yao, Jin-shui
Zhao, Hui
coredata.dc:description: Ascorbic acid (AA) is very important for maintaining the normal physiological processes of the organism. In order to overcome the shortcomings of existing methods, such as lower sensitivity, complex operations and expensive instruments, it is urgent to develop a simple and rapid method for the determination of AA in the field of food and pharmaceutical preparations. Conjugated polymer nanoparticles (CPNs) have lately aroused wide concerns because of excellent optical properties, biocompatibility and stability. In our work, a CPNsPBOC-COOH fluorescence platform based on Poly [3-{2,5-bis (2-ethyl-hexyloxy)]-phenyl}-vinyl}-9-octyl-carbazole] (PBOC) and Polystyrene-maleic anhydride (PSMA), was prepared for the quantitative detection of AA. Experimental results were showed that this platform was highly selective and sensitive to AA. The concentration of AA detected by the fluorescence platform has a linear relationship in the range of 0.57–17.10 μM. The limit of detection (LOD) is 10 nM, which is lower than other methods reported. More interestingly, the fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH can be controlled by logic gates with Fe3+ and AA. Finally, the designed fluorescence platform has been successfully applied to the analysis of AA in actual samples (vitamin C tablets, mouse serum and plasma), indicating the designed platform with a good feasibility.
coredata.dcterms:subject: CPNs
AA
Fluorescence platform
Detection

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Fluorescence detection of AA by the platform (CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+)
The detection of AA by fluorescence platform in actual samples
The design and characterization of CPNsPBOC-COOH
The parameter optimization of fluorescence platform between CPNsPBOC-COOH and Fe3+
Fluorescence platform for the detection of AA
Fluorescence platform applied in the detection of AA in actual samples

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: Natural Science Foundation of Shandong Province
National Natural Science Foundation of China
Sichuan Provincial Youth Science and Technology Fund

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The (a) (b) AFM images and (c) SEM image of CPNsPBOC-COOH.

Particle size distribution of CPNsPBOC-COOH (1 μg/mL) in the different concentrations of Fe3+ by DLS. (a) Fe3+: 0 μM; (b) Fe3+: 0.4 μM; (c) Fe3+: 5 μM. The TEM images of CPNsPBOC-COOH (1 μg/mL) in different concentrations of Fe3+ (d) Fe3+: 0 μM; (e) Fe3+: 5 μM; and (f) AA: 10 μM.

(a)Fluorescence emission spectra of CPNsPBOC-COOH (1 μg/mL) with the addition of different concentrations of Fe3+(Inset: the fluorescence trend at 550 nm); (b) A linear relationship between 0.5–3 μM at 550 nm; (c)Fluorescence emission spectra of CPNsPBOC-COOH in the presence of different metal cations; (d)The selective fluorescence histogram of CPNsPBOC-COOH to Fe3+at 550 nm. Where F0 represents the integrated fluorescence intensity of CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+, F is the fluorescence intensity in the presence of AA, and X is the concentrations of AA.

(a)Fluorescence emission spectra of CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ (1 μg/mL; 20 μM) with the addition of different concentrations of AA; (b) a linear relationship between the fluorescence at 550 nm and the concentrations of AA (0.57–18.24 μM)); (c)Fluorescence emission spectra of CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ in the presence of different disturbed substances (10 μM); (d)The selective fluorescence histogram of CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ to AA at 550 nm.
Analytical results of AA in actual samples (Vc chewable tablets, Vc effervescent tablets, mouse serum and plasma).
The preparation process and sensing mechanism of fluorescence platform.
Comparing this method with other reported fluorescence detection methods of AA.

head.ce:title: The sensitive “Turn-on” fluorescence platform of ascorbic acid based on conjugated polymer nanoparticles

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Materials Science and Engineering
Qilu University of Technology (Shandong Academy of Sciences)

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Ascorbic acid (AA) is very important for maintaining the normal physiological processes of the organism. In order to overcome the shortcomings of existing methods, such as lower sensitivity, complex operations and expensive instruments, it is urgent to develop a simple and rapid method for the determination of AA in the field of food and pharmaceutical preparations. Conjugated polymer nanoparticles (CPNs) have lately aroused wide concerns because of excellent optical properties, biocompatibility and stability. In our work, a CPNsPBOC-COOH fluorescence platform based on Poly [3-{2,5-bis (2-ethyl-hexyloxy)]-phenyl}-vinyl}-9-octyl-carbazole] (PBOC) and Polystyrene-maleic anhydride (PSMA), was prepared for the quantitative detection of AA. Experimental results were showed that this platform was highly selective and sensitive to AA. The concentration of AA detected by the fluorescence platform has a linear relationship in the range of 0.57–17.10 μM. The limit of detection (LOD) is 10 nM, which is lower than other methods reported. More interestingly, the fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH can be controlled by logic gates with Fe3+ and AA. Finally, the designed fluorescence platform has been successfully applied to the analysis of AA in actual samples (vitamin C tablets, mouse serum and plasma), indicating the designed platform with a good feasibility.

ce:section.ce:para: Conjugated polymers (CPs) are very important members of the fluorescent material family [1]. Firstly, the conjugated delocalization [2,3] and rigid structure of the main chain of CPs supply excellent fluorescence properties [4]. Secondly, it has better absorption coefficients and higher fluorescence quantum yields [5,6], which are all due to the large π-electron delocalization skeleton on the conjugated polymer structure [7,8]. Finally, CPs can be "molecular wire" [9,10], which makes its fluorescence signal magnify several times or even tens of times than some traditional probes [11,12]. Although long flexible side chains were introduced into the main chains of CPs in order to improve their solubility, there are still many defects, such as poor light stability and toxicity, which still need to be improved. Conjugated polymer nanoparticles (CPNs) prepared from CPs have developed rapidly in recent years due to their improved water solubility and easy surface modification. CPNs [13,14] can be prepared by the methods of microemulsion [15,16], nanoprecipitation [17,18] and self-assembly [19,20], and gradually become a research hotspot in the fields of biosensors [21–24] because of the advantages such as easy preparation and separation, high brightness, good light stability and low toxicity, and so on.

Ascorbic acid (AA), also known as vitamin C (Vc), is one of the important vitamins to maintain the normal physiological process of the body [25,26]. AA is a nutrient that can’t be synthesized and must be taken from foods to maintain the necessary content of the human body [27]. According to the standard of Chinese Pharmacopoeia 2010, the qualified range for the determination of AA in VC drugs should be 93%–107% of the labeled amount. Stability is an important basis for quality control, safety and effectiveness of drug. The lack of AA can lead to scurvy, but the excessive intake of AA can also be harmful to body [28]. Therefore, it is vital to analyze the content of AA in foods and pharmaceutical preparations by simple and rapid methods [29]. Recently, researchers have developed a series of identification methods, such as titration analysis [30,31], spectrophotometry [32,33], electrochemical methods [34–36], etc. In comparison to other methods, fluorescence sensor or platform has obvious advantages due to its high sensitivity and portability. However, there are few reports of fluorescence sensors for AA detection based on CPNs. And it is difficult to accurately detect the content of AA by the common fluorescence probes, so the fluorescence probes with the high sensitivity and stability are expected to be designed.

Herein, we designed a fluorescence platform based on CPNsPBOC (PBOC: poly [3-{2,5-bis (2-ethyl-hexyloxy)]-phenyl}-vinyl}-9-octyl-carbazole]) and Fe3+ for the quantitative detection of AA. CPNsPBOC-COOH was prepared by the nanoprecipitation method with PSMA (Polystyrene-maleic anhydride) and PBOC synthetized in our previous work. Compared to CPNsPBOC, CPNsPBOC-COOH has been improved in water solubility and excellent light stability. CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ is an ideal fluorescence platform based on that Fe3+ can aggregate CPNsPBOC-COOH and quench the fluorescence. The addition of AA will reduce the aggregation and recover the fluorescence. It has been proved by experiments that the fluorescence platform has high selectivity and sensitivity to AA with the better limit of detection (LOD) than those in other methods reported. Finally, the designed platform was successfully applied to analyze AA in actual samples (Vc tablets, mouse serum and plasma).

PBOC was synthesized according to the previous work of our research group [37], and the synthetic route was described in the supporting information. The number average molecular weight of PSMA was 18,000 with the structure units of 202, and its degree of polymerization was 864. The PBOC and PSMA were separately dissolved in THF to prepare a 1 mg/mL stock solution. The mixed solution (10 mL) consisting of PBOC (50 μg/mL) and PSMA (10 μg/mL) in THF was sonicated for 1 min to form a homogeneous solution. Then, 20 mL of ultrapure water was quickly added under ultrasonic conditions and sonicated for 15 min. The mixed solution was removed THF by rotary evaporation and concentrated to 10 mL. Finally, it was filtered through a filter (0.2 μm) to collect the supernatant (CPNsPBOC-COOH).

In order to establish an optimized sensing platform, some experiments were carried out to get the optimized parameters. Different volumes of Fe(NO3)3·6H2O (0–20 μM) solution were added to CPNsPBOC-COOH solution (1 μg/mL), and the mixed solution was standing for 10 min at room temperature, followed by fluorescence tests. And the fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH (1 μg/mL) in the presence of different metal cations (Ag+, Al3+, Ca2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Hg2+, Na+, Ni2+, Pd2+, Zn2+, Fe2+, Fe3+) with a concentration of 5 μΜ was investigated. The fluorescence spectra were got at an excitation wavelength of 430 nm with a slit of 5 nm.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence detection of AA by the platform (CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+)
ce:section.ce:para: In a typical experiment for detecting AA, different concentrations of AA solution (0–18.24 μM), CPNsPBOC-COOH (1 μg/mL) and Fe(NO3)3·6H2O (5 μM) solution were mixed. The mixture was incubated at room temperature for 30 min, and the fluorescence emission spectra of the mixture were recorded for quantitative analysis. The selectivity was evaluated by the addition of different interfering substances (10 μM), including glutamate, glucose, glycine, calcium chloride, alanine, and cysteine. The parameters of fluorescence tests are the same as the previous settings.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The detection of AA by fluorescence platform in actual samples
ce:section.ce:para: Every piece of Vc was ground and dissolved in ultrapure water. The mixture was sonicated for 10 min to dissolve sufficiently. The solutions were diluted with ultrapure water to get different concentrations of AA that reached the linear range of the standard curve. Then, different concentrations of AA were added to the diluted solution. The added samples were allowed to react with the sensing solution at room temperature for 10 min. This platform was successfully applied to the detection of AA in actual samples. Mouse serum and plasma samples were centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 10 min. CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ solutions were added into the diluted supernatant. Subsequent operations were identical to the determination of AA in the aqueous solution.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The design and characterization of CPNsPBOC-COOH
ce:section.ce:para: CPNsPBOC-COOH were synthesized by the nanoprecipitation method using PBOC and PSMA as described above. The best concentration ratio of PBOC and PSMA was obtained through careful selection. The aim was to prepare CPNsPBOC-COOH with small particle size and uniform distribution. And the specific data was displayed in supporting information. The good result was obtained on condition of PBOC (50 μg/mL) and PSMA (10 μg/mL). The fluorescent quantum yield of CPNsPBOC-COOH in water was measured using coumarin 307 (Φ = 0.56, in methanol) standard as reference [38,39] with a value of 47%. The calculation details are described in the supporting information. The synthetic route and sensing mechanism of CPNsPBOC-COOH are shown in Scheme 1 . Two factors were considered for the preparation of CPNsPBOC-COOH. Firstly, although CPs have been extensively studied, their solubility and biocompatibility in water still need to be improved. The long alkyl side chain of PBOC (Fig. S1) can effectively improve the solubility. In addition, PSMA was hydrolyzed to supply carboxyl groups, which can improve the water solubility and biocompatibility of CPNsPBOC. Secondly, the carboxyl groups on the nanoparticles provide active sites to link metal ions and the possibility to construct the sensing platform. The Zeta potential data of different kinds of nanoparticles are shown in Fig. S2. The Zeta potential of CPNsPBOC-COOH (1 μg/mL) was reduced to −14.1 mV in comparison with that of pure CPNsPBOC (−4.69 mV) (1 μg/mL), which demonstrated that carboxylic acid groups on the surface of CPNsPBOC have been introduced. The particle size and morphology of CPNsPBOC-COOH were characterized by atomic force microscopy (AFM) in Fig. 1 a and b, scanning electron microscope (SEM) in Fig. 1c dynamic light scattering (DLS) in Fig. 2 a–c and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) in Fig. 2d–f. The morphology of CPNsPBOC-COOH is relatively uniform and approximately spherical by AFM (Fig. 1a and b), SEM (Fig. 1c) and TEM (Fig. 2d). The average particle size of CPNsPBOC-COOH is 28.03 nm by DLS in Fig. 2a.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The parameter optimization of fluorescence platform between CPNsPBOC-COOH and Fe3+
ce:section.ce:para: The optical properties of CPNsPBOC and CPNsPBOC-COOH were investigated by UV absorption and fluorescence emission spectra. As showed in Fig. S3, there were slightly blue-shift in the UV and fluorescence emission spectra of CPNsPBOC and CPNsPBOC-COOH. This indicated that the introduction of carboxyl groups has a relatively small effect on the optical properties of pure PBOC. And one can see that CPNsPBOC-COOH have a strong emission peak at 550 nm.

The fluorescence spectra and their change trend of CPNsPBOC-COOH (1 μg/mL) with the titration of Fe3+ (0–20 μM) are shown in Fig. 3 a. It could be seen clearly from the inset of Fig. 3a that the fluorescence intensity of CPNsPBOC-COOH decreased with the increased concentrations of Fe3+. When the concentration of Fe3+ was increased to 5 μM, the fluorescence intensity tended to steady. Which implied that the carboxyl groups on CPNsPBOC-COOH have completely coordinated with Fe3+ [40,41]. Fig. 3b displayed that a good linear fitting was found between the fluorescence intensity of CPNsPBOC-COOH and the concentrations of Fe3+ from 0.5 μM to 3 μM. The fluorescence platform was obtained based on the optimized condition of CPNsPBOC-COOH (1 μg/mL) and Fe3+ (1 μg/mL, 5 μM). The fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH was quenched and the quenching efficiency reached up to nearly 90%.

Moreover, we studied the fluorescence interference of CPNsPBOC-COOH (1 μg/mL) from different metal cations (Ag+, Al3+, Ca2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Hg2+, Na+, Ni2+, Pd2+, Zn2+, Fe2+) with a concentration of 5 μM, and they were shown in Fig. 3c and d. The results displayed that the fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH was quenched by Fe3+ and Hg2+. But the quenching efficiency in the present Fe3+ is the best, so CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ was used as the sensing platform for the further application.

In order to explore the interaction between CPNsPBOC-COOH and Fe3+, the particle sizes of CPNsPBOC-COOH before and after the addition of Fe3+ were measured by DLS. Fig. 2b and c displayed that the particle size of CPNsPBOC-COOH obviously increased after the addition of Fe3+. The average particle size of CPNsPBOC-COOH in the absence of Fe3+ was 28.03 nm (Fig. 2a). The value became 90.27 nm after adding 0.4 μM of Fe3+ (Fig. 2b). Furthermore, it reached 2123.04 nm when the concentration of Fe3+ was 5 μM (Fig. 2c). TEM images (Fig. 2e) intuitively displayed that the aggregation of CPNsPBOC-COOH caused by Fe3+. The possible reason can be attributed to the aggregation of nanoparticle because of coordination effect between Fe3+ and CPNsPBOC-COOH [42]. Moreover, the Zeta potentials of CPNsPBOC-COOH in the absence and presence of Fe3+ were shown in Fig. S2. The value was raised from −14.1 mV to 27.4 mV after adding Fe3+ (5 μM), which proved that there are electrostatic interaction and coordination effect between Fe3+ and CPNsPBOC-COOH. The similar conclusions were reported in other researches [43]. The conclusion was consistent with the results of the above fluorescence.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence platform for the detection of AA
ce:section.ce:para:
Since AA is well-known importance to human health, it become a hotspot to detect AA in food and humans. Herein, we use CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ as sensing platform for detecting AA. As a general weak reductant, AA can reduce Fe3+ to Fe2+ by the redox reaction [26,44]. Thus, the coordination between CPNsPBOC-COOH and Fe3+ was broken, meanwhile Fe2+ could not quench the fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH, so the fluorescence was recovered. Under the optimal conditions (CPNsPBOC-COOH: 1 μg/mL; Fe3+: 5 μM), the Zeta potential of the platform is reduced from 27.4 mV to −16.7 mV by adding AA (10 μM) (Fig. S2). And TEM image in Fig. 2f shown that the aggregation of CPNsPBOC-COOH decreased significantly after AA was added. The fluorescence recovery experiments of CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ in the presence of different concentrations of AA were studied. Fig. 4 a displayed that the fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ gradually enhanced with the increased concentration of AA from 0.57 μM to 17.10 μM. After 18.24 μM of AA, the fluorescence intensities tend to be stable. A good linear fitting between the fluorescence intensities and the concentrations of AA (0.57–17.10 μM) was obtained in Fig. 4b. The fitting equation is shown as follows:
F–F
0 = 29.79 X + 83.03 (R2 = 0.9903)
where F
0 represents the integrated fluorescence intensity of CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ , F is the fluorescence intensity in the presence of AA, and X is the concentrations of AA.

The LOD was calculated according to the method reported in the reference with a value of 10 nM [45,46]. Compared to the linear range and detection limits of other materials for AA detection by the fluorescence method (Table 1 ) [47–51], the detection limits and linear range in this work are very comparable.

Moreover, we studied also the fluorescence response of the platform after the addition of different possible disturbed substances (Glu, Glc, Gly, CaCl2, Ala, Cys) with a concentration of 10 μM. Fig. 4c and d shown that CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ exhibited a higher selectivity for AA.

Furthermore, the fluorescence cycle test (five times) was carried out by continuously adding Fe3+ (5 μM) and AA (10 μM) to the CPNsPBOC-COOH solution and shown in Fig. S6. The results indicated that the fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH can be recycled and controlled by logic gates using Fe3+ and AA. The interaction mechanism can be attributed to the aggregation behavior of CPNsPBOC-COOH in the presence of Fe3+ to cause fluorescence quenching. The addition of AA will bind to Fe3+ and reduce the aggregation to recover the fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH. The quenching efficiencies of the fluorescence platform are in range from 84% to 98%, and the recovery efficiencies are in range from 88% to 94%.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence platform applied in the detection of AA in actual samples
ce:section.ce:para: The feasibility of fluorescence platform in actual sample analysis was evaluated by testing different concentrations of AA in Vc chewable and Vc effervescent tablets. The experimental results are shown in Fig. 5 . The instructions of Vc drug revealed AA content specifications in Vc chewable and effervescent tablets (100 mg AA per gram), so we configure different concentrations (5 μM and 10 μM) of AA according to the content. Vc tablets used for each measurement are randomly selected. Then, different concentrations of AA (5 μM and 8 μM) were added to the samples, respectively. And the standard addition experiments were carried out. The fluorescence results were almost identical to the specifications in the manual, and the recovery rates ranged from 93.3% to 103.1%. This verified the accuracy of the content of the drug, and proved that the designed fluorescence platform was suitable for the quantitative determination of AA in actual samples.

In order to verify the potential in the biological field, this platform has been applied to detect AA in the environment of mouse serum and plasma (Fig. 5). Since the purchased mice serum and plasma do not contain AA, its fluorescence tests are carried out by an external added way (10 μM and 15 μM). The recovery is between 93.4% and 100.2%. The results are satisfactory, which shows that the platform has good application prospects for biosensors.

In summary, a "turn on" fluorescence platform was constructed based on the fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH quenched by Fe3+ for the quantitative determination of AA. The sensing mechanism was investigated by fluorescence spectra, DLS and Zeta potentials. The results showed that CPNsPBOC-COOH would aggregate in the presence of Fe3+ to induce the fluorescence quenching, then the fluorescence would be recovered after the addition of AA because AA reduced Fe3+ to Fe2+ and declined the aggregation. The fluorescence platform of CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ was highly selective and sensitive for AA with a lower LOD of 10 nM. A cyclic logic gate was established by the fluorescence of CPNsPBOC-COOH quenched with Fe3+ and recovered after adding AA. Furthermore, CPNsPBOC-COOH-Fe3+ was successfully applied to the determination of AA in actual samples with satisfactory results. This type of fluorescence platform may supply further application advantages for the efficient detection of AA.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This project was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (NSFC 51403111, 11774188), Shandong Provincial Natural Science Foundation PhD Foundation (ZR2017BH053), and Sichuan Provincial Science and Technology Innovation Talents (2018RZ0113). We are particularly grateful to the funding supporting from Qilu University of Technology for talents.

ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.aca.2019.10.076.
